title: Garlicky Indulgent Norwegian Cheese Kjøttkaker
description: A rich and savory Norwegian dish featuring yellow masarepa and a decadent cheese pull, perfect for a special occasion or a indulgent treat.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Prepare the Binchotan Charcoal by soaking it in water for at least 30 minutes before use.
In a large pan, cook the bacon over medium heat until crispy, then set aside.
In the same pan, cook the ground beef until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the onion and garlic to the pan and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the Gruyère cheese until melted and well combined with the beef mixture.
Add the cooked bacon back into the pan and stir to combine.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked beef mixture, egg, salt, and black pepper.
Add the Yellow Masarepa to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Use your hands to shape the mixture into 6-8 patties.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and cook the patties for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until nicely browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for an additional 10-12 minutes, or until cooked through.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let the Kjøttkaker rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
